assumptions: The developer has an existing web project folder containing HTML, CSS, JavaScript, or framework source.
The developer has a supported browser and reliable network connection.
For the logged-in drag-and-drop candidate, the developer has a Netlify account and can access the intended team. An anonymous branch exists but behaves differently for unbuilt source.
If the folder is dropped anonymously, it must already be built because Netlify publishes anonymous files as-is.

developer_goal: Publish an existing web project on Netlify and open its live preview URL.

first_success.outcome: Dropped project is deployed and available at its live netlify.app URL.
first_success.observable_signal: Opening the Preview/live URL ending in netlify.app serves the published site.

route_selection.surface: Publish an existing folder through logged-in Netlify Drop
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official first-deploy tutorial path without duplicating steps or splitting form fields into separate required path steps.
route_selection.selected: Publish an existing folder through logged-in Netlify Drop

candidates.name: Publish an existing folder through logged-in Netlify Drop
candidates.reason: Netlify Start from files / Drop path publishes a local folder to a live netlify.app URL. Removed a duplicated copy of the same path that previously inflated step counts.

required_path.action: Open the Netlify documentation home and select Create a new project.
Open Choose your path and select Start from files for a complete drag-and-drop candidate.
required_path.observable_result: The page explains the account, Drop publisher, folder, and preview URL sequence.

required_path.action: Prepare the local project folder to deploy.
required_path.observable_result: The folder is unzipped and includes the project files; for anonymous publishing it already contains built files.

required_path.action: Log in to the Netlify account and select the team that should manage the project.
required_path.observable_result: The team-owned Projects page or Netlify Drop publisher can be opened.

required_path.action: Open the Netlify Drop publisher or scroll to the drag-and-drop section at the bottom of the team's Projects page.
required_path.observable_result: The folder drop target is ready.

required_path.action: Drag the project folder into the Deploy zone.
required_path.observable_result: The upload and deploy process begins.

required_path.action: For a logged-in source folder, detect the framework, run the build if needed, and publish the result; for prebuilt output, publish the supplied files.
required_path.observable_result: The project dashboard shows a Preview URL ending in netlify.app.

required_path.action: Open the Preview URL shown in the project dashboard.
required_path.observable_result: The intended project is visible at the Preview URL and the official guide says it is now published on the web.

prerequisites.description: For the managed drag-and-drop route, a Netlify account authenticated into the team that should own the project. The public docs link to signup but do not reconstruct its current atomic screens or verification order.
A current Chromium-based browser is recommended for drag-and-drop troubleshooting.
A local web project folder. A ZIP archive must be unzipped first.
If deploying anonymously or using manual deployment without a build, the folder must contain already built output. A logged-in drop can let Netlify detect a framework and run its build.
A current Free credit-based plan is sufficient for a first production deployment while credits remain. A production deployment consumes 15 credits.

external_gates.description: Netlify presents several peer starting routes and does not select a universal default.
The correct folder depends on whether the developer is logged in. Anonymous drops publish files as-is, while logged-in drops can detect and build a framework project.
The managed route requires login, but the linked signup flow is not atomically documented on the public Start pages.
The docs say deploys under 50 MB work best and that individual files over 10 MB can cause a stuck deploy.
Network reliability, browser version, and local free memory can block upload. The troubleshooting baseline suggests roughly four times the uncompressed project size in free memory.
Netlify may need to detect, build, and publish the project before the Preview URL is available. No typical duration is documented for this route.
A production deployment consumes 15 credits. The Free plan includes 300 monthly credits with a hard limit.

platform_outcomes.description: Dropped project is deployed and available at its live netlify.app URL.

excluded.reason: Path compacted to the official first-success tutorial structure.

sources.title: Netlify Documentation
evidence_supported: Peer AI, Git and drag-and-drop routes

sources.title: Deploy from AI code generation tool
evidence_supported: Official drag-and-drop recommendation for AI-generated projects

sources.title: Netlify Glossary for Beginners
evidence_supported: Instant and seconds-level Netlify Drop time claim

uncertainties.question: Which of AI agent, Git repository, framework starter, template, drag and drop, or CLI is Netlify's single canonical first route?
uncertainties.impact: Different entrants face materially different plans, credentials, authorization, local tooling, and first-success terminals.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: The documentation home and Choose your path explicitly present multiple routes and do not rank one as universal.

uncertainties.question: What is the current atomic Netlify account signup and verification sequence?
uncertainties.impact: The logged-in route can contain additional fields, authentication choices, email verification, terms, or team creation before the first deploy.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: The official quickstarts link to the application signup page but the public documentation inspected does not reconstruct its current steps.

uncertainties.question: Does the current logged-in Drop publisher always run a framework build for unbuilt source?
uncertainties.impact: The required local preparation and wait or failure states differ between source and output folders.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Choose your path says logged-in drops can detect and build, while Create deploys states that manual deployment does not run a build command.
